Attorney William Murphy was my voice when it felt like I didn't have one. After experiencing a car accident and an injury that did not appear until the next day, I was following protocol in the effort to get the proper medical attention and care. I had insurance, however, the other party in the accident had limited insurance. I began experiencing issues on deductibles, and payments for medical care and ongoing treatment. A  concerned professional suggested Attorney Murphy. As soon as I called and met with Mr. Murphy my mind was put at ease. I knew that someone else, who was capable, was going to be my voice. I did not have to argue and stress over the care that my son and I needed. After talking with my attorney, I was able to think clearly, and make the medical decisions necessary to maintain our health and well-being. I have never felt more thankful for someone in our lives, as I am for William Murphy. He cared about us as individuals, and our quality of life. If you are reading this then I believe that you may be in need of some good advice. Please call this office. I believe that they will do everything humanely possible to help.

reviews.text: I'd give this law firm zero stars if that was an option!  I had a phone consultation scheduled at 11am to go over some estate planning and when I didn't receive a call by 11:15am, I decided to call the office.  I was placed on hold while the receptionist (Dawn) was reaching out to Sarah Bolen to find out why she didn't keep the appointment.  After 5 minutes of waiting on hold, I ended the call.  I received a call back from Dawn about 2 minutes later and she indicated that Sarah Bolen had bronchitis and therefore needed to reschedule.  I asked why nobody called me in advance because Sarah must have known that she had bronchitis prior to that particular moment.  I was told that she was having a "coughing fit" but Dawn could text her back to see if the fit was over.  Hmmm... I'm no lawyer but I'm not stupid and know when I'm being lied to.  VERY UNPROFESSIONAL!
reviews.ownerResponse: Hello Big Worm- we apologize that Attorney Bolen was experiencing an unexpected health issue at the time of your scheduled phone consultation. We can assure you that it is not in the organization or attorney’s best interest to willingly or intentionally avoid new client consultations. Given the unfortunate timing of this extremely uncommon event, our office manager reached out to you to attempt to reschedule your appointment, and Attorney Bolen, while still ill, did reach out to you personally to apologize and further explain the actual details of the situation. We are sorry about the timing of this unexpected event. We wish you the best, Big Worm.

I had to work out custody for my son and I used a lawyer here. He was very nice but I feel like I was constantly being told to settle and nothing could change the fact that I would have to eventually go to 50-50, regardless of the fact that my son’s father does not have a job, doesn’t support our son at all, barely sees him, and he has had police reports due to assault. I was told a judge wouldn’t care and felt a lot of pressure to just move to 50-50 even though I have expressed that I didn’t believe it was in my son’s best interest. After talking to several people, I finally decided to bring up my concerns to Tara Sharp, who owns the firm. She was incredibly rude and mentioned over and over again that I should have said something sooner, insinuating that it was my fault for waiting. I didn’t say anything sooner since I am trusting my lawyer to look out for my best interests, especially when the retainer is a few thousand dollars. I wasn’t even planning on leaving a review but she was so rude to me that I left 1 star. She then emailed me regarding the review multiple times and I felt like I was being bullied into changing my review. She had previously emailed saying if I needed help in the future, she would do it for a “reduced rate” but after I left the review, she made a snide remark saying, “ I was willing to give you a reduced rate for your troubles, but obviously that is not good enough for you.”. I never once asked for my money back, or for a reduced rate so that comment was completely uncalled for. And honestly, I had an issue with my representation so a reduced rate really isn’t good enough. If you’re looking for lawyers who will fight for your needs and be respectful, I suggest finding a different firm. I’ve already reached out to a few and my experience has already been 100 times better

reviews.text:
Robert Soltis charged me for simply replying to his emails! He was already pre-paid for four hours just to settle a $1,000 debt in exchange for negative tradeline removal.
Not only did he not do that, or even ASK for that as I requested but to get more money out of me, he charged me for "responding" to emails! I only needed to reach a settlement agreement for a $1,000 debt. I still had to pay $600 PLUS an additional $225.00 (in addition to the four hours that he was already paid) to him personally.  I could have settled this debt myself for FREE! I ired him in hopes that he could negotiate the negative trade line in from my credit in exchange for the settlement amount.  Robert NEVER even tried. He asked me if I'd pay them $600 versus $1,000(that's all I actually owed anyways). When I asked about the trade line request- he just said, "I can't do that."
I would NEVER recommend Robert Soltis. It's really sad because I actually have had the pleasure of caring for a loved one of the Kriekard family. She was a woman of integrity and character. Based on my connection alone, I would have been comfortable partnering with this firm to help me with the population that I serve.  I'd hate to think that THIS lack of integrity is a reflection of every attorney involved with this practice- so, I can only speak for Soltis. His assistant is equally as hardened. I'm sure she shares his moral compass for doing business...
I even gave them the opportunity to "make this right," but they were more interested in swindling $225 more dollars from me than doing the right thing! So, charging a single mom an EXTRA $225.00 because they know that they can is horrible. $225 is a drop in the bucket for a firm of that size, but I guess if you do that enough you can pad your pockets pretty well.  If the work  that I asked for was done and id I genuinely owed the "extra" money, I wouldn't be taking the time to write this not so positive review. I just think that it's important to WARN others who will be vulnerable while looking for help.
In conclusion: I was not informed of how my time was being used. I was not informed that I was being charged for replying to an emails that he initiated!  There was multiple email exchanges that could have been clustered into one to communicate the same information.  But instead, he milked my time! Charged me $56.25 per email!  It's just sad. It's expensive enough to hire an attorney when you're not rich.  To have the attorney take advantage of you that's supposed to help you is just disheartening. Not to mention that I work with a population that actually NEED legal representation and actually have the money to afford them.  He just lost a whole lot more money for the firm than he gained over a $225.00 scam! I'm sure those that read this will scroll to the next attorney instead of them because of how they conducted business; costing them even more money...
For the record, I paid the extra $225 although I don't feel that I owed it because I never used 4 hours of time knowingly.  It's not like I can afford to fight the office that I hired to help me.
So, unless you're rich- STAY AWAY FROM THIS ATTORNEY.
Exceptionally unethical business practices. Will screw you over then demand insane rates, find anyone else to take your case if you're not looking to lose miserably.
Gotta love companies that randomly spam call you trying to get you too give them your money. Run fast and for.

The whole dealing with this law firm caused me to have a mental breakdown. Using them to deal with estates and deaths of loved ones is absolute hell.

reviews.text:
"If your child does not come with you for your parenting time, then you pick your child up, throw them over your shoulder, strap them in your car, and shut them in their room at your house." This was the advice Rex Brueggemann gave me when, in 2022, I engaged with him to represent me in the separation from my non-married partner of over 15 years.

Rex consistently gave me advice that was counter to my interests as a father/parent and as a business owner.

His tactic seems to rely on heavily pressuring his clients to "settle", much to the favor of the opposing party, while not acting on my requests that would balance the end results of the ruling order.

In the end, I had to terminate my engagement with Rex and hire another attorney to represent me. Hiring Rex was a waste of time and money, and it cost me significant leverage with gaining equal parenting time for my children. In the end, he wouldn't even refund the balance of my retainer, adding to the ridiculousness of this experience.

I only hope this review helps others save time and money and hardship.

My wife and I used Rex for our estate planning. He did such an outstanding job that I referred friends to him when they were in need of assistance. Rex is always available for questions and simplifies complex matters. I am grateful for his expertise and kind soul!
Had a Awesome day!!@

Rex charges more than what his value is.  He is very nonchalant and laid back. It seems like he does not even want to do the minimum for the retainer fee he charges.   He will nickel and dime you and you will not get any of the retainer money back even though he may say in the initial consultation that you will get a refund of what's not used. He does not give you the bake if what he charges you.
Straight and to the point, they know what they're talking about. It was a pleasure doing business with you!

I began my case with a firm in Portage that I let go due to improper representation, and was referred to Brueggemann and Sharma by a well respected Kalamazoo firm.  I was told they were honest, cared about their clients and were dependable Attorneys.  Rex represented me in a Federal Divorce, which wasn't easy.  My experience with the firm was excellent, as Rex was brutally honest in telling me what I could expect, thereby eliminating a battle that would amount to costly fees and limited results.  He and his technical staff, Marcia Cornell, were prompt in getting the required paperwork filed on time, keeping me informed regarding all matters related to the case, and simply communicating.  I could not have asked for a better Attorney, as he was not only prompt and efficient, but well versed in Federal retirement and divorce law.  He is someone who prepares diligently for a case, not ten minutes prior to representation.  I am grateful he took my case that was not his originally, and turned something wrong into something right.  Most importantly, it was refreshing to work with an Attorney that is honest, has great character, and genuinely cares about his clients.  I would recommend him to anyone.  Thank you Brueggeman and Sharma!
They both are wonderful lawyers. I've used them both during different cases and years. I also had my son use them. I wouldn't go anywhere else.
Namita is incredibly patient, respectful, professional and thorough. I would recommend her support to anyone in need of family law services.
Rex Brueggeman is A very respectable guy. If you need a divorce, custody, wills and trusts attorney if you give him a call.  He is super personable and really likes to help people out.
Still waiting on response from them.
I appreciate them "teaching" and not just "telling".  I feel educated and better equipped to make good decisions regarding my legal matters.
I found out for them to finalize everything after my mom past away they wanted to charge $8.500 to finalize. I was able to get it done for roughly for $3.500.

We had some stressful times the past few years with involvement in settling  another family trust.  It was a surprise to us that the property and family home were not in the trust.  This was my realization that it's very important to have your trust completed by a law firm to which they specialize in just that.  Attorney Henke gave us great legal advise to addressing some new factors within our trust.  It's definitely a secure feeling that you have professional, knowledgeable people working for your best interest.

Great advice from James Henke.

I will say, I have never felt so disrespected and treated like a scolded child as I did in the conversation that I had with Mr. Trainor. I have never had an experience with a professional attorney that made me feel like I was incompetent. I want to say that if this is how you treat your customers, I surely do not want to be a part of you gaining from my case. We do not need financial gain from this case. We know that there may be a need for extended coverage with our insurance company, we know that there is a possible suit. I am not going to feel bullied by an attorney to get my son or daughter into medical treatment that we are not sure they need quite yet. To me, this is fraudulent and not necessary just for financial gain. While you were criticizing my phone answering skills, you were not able to answer the question that I asked you several times which was, what exactly does your team need from me? There was no answer, I asked your paralegal the same question without an answer. If there is criticism for not answering my phone, perhaps there should be a reasons for a phone call? I want to also say, I am a very successful businesswoman, respected, treated well by colleagues and have a program that is so successful. I should not as a customer feel I have to say these things, no one should be treated as I was during that phone call.
Highly recommended. Always keep us updated and have a good line of communication open. They have helped us find qualified physicians in our area.
Will elaborate later and update my review. Not a great law firm. Wouldn’t recommend them.

I recently received the assistance of Bill Webster, one of the lawyers with this firm. I had been fighting for disability some time and he came to me as a recommendation from a different lawyer.

He was amazing! He walked me through the process. I already had a loss in the past with a different lawyer, so I was going to try to do this alone. The decision to work with Bill was the best one I could have made! He knows the local judges and has the right insights to help guide us in the right direction.

Bill stood out by requesting a form to be filled out by a doctor that knows my case well. This is something I never heard before with anyone else. Having a supportive doctor who was able to answer his carefully curated questions was a real game changer.

He knew the right questions that would garner the appropriate insight into my capabilities. These are the type of questions that are generally not answered clearly in medical records alone.

I will be recommending him to anyone I know in the future who may need help with their own case. Having him in my corner was exactly what I needed to make a difference.

Thank you, Bill, for being more than a lawyer. Thank you for caring about my struggle and giving your best effort!

I could not recommend the guy I met (Mitch Moldavan) with for potential help my divorce hearing any less. If I would haven taken his advice I’d have been screwed. So I let him go and said I didn’t need his services. His recommendation couldn’t have been any worse; moreover, I did what he told me I shouldn’t do, got the perfect results. THEN! This guy walked in in the middle of the hearing claiming he “forgot his jacket”, stayed for the rest of the hearing, tried to make like he was supposed to be there, AND THEN LATER TRIED TO CHARGE ME FOR IT! Every lawyer I spoke with after this told me that this is among some of the most unprofessional behavior they’d ever heard — so I saved all our corresponding emails for anyone looking at who NOT to hire. This guy is the WORST.

Update: HOWEVER! The firm itself handled his misconduct very respectfully and were very apologetic for what he did. That much I’m very grateful for. So wherever Moldavan ended up gets my  sincerest sympathy.

I hired this firm in 2016 to assist with executing my late father's estate. Thomas Becker was recommended to me by LegalShield after an accident where I hit a car that was illegally parked on the highway.

Several other friends including another lawyer told me that the entire situation sounded suspicious. Thomas Becker recommended that I plea down even though the other vehicle was turned off on the pavement of the highway. And I was positive that they wouldn't show up in court specifically because They did not want to provide any information to me. Mr Becker advised me that this was a no win situation and not even to try.

The person in that vehicle decided to sue me for medical expenses. I believe that they fought to get everything that they could out of my insurance company and not because they needed it.I believe that this additional expense Is a large part of the reason why I cannot afford to drive anymore.  My insurance has effectively increased from a couple hundred dollars a month for full coverage (A little expensive in Michigan but not incredibly so) to almost a thousand per month.

Thomas Becker does not care about you. He only cares about his own bottom line. Or perhaps he was paid off. All I know is that I wouldn't recommend him to anybody.

Then we drove by their law office about 4 weeks ago I'm Richmond, MI only to see a "for lease" sign covering their name sign.  Facebook gave no updates except other people having the same issue, in the middle of a case and now they can't even be found. My first case with them was great, in 6 months everything was settled.  Both Tom and Jammie were helpful and knowledgeable about my case and the law.  I am not sure what happened exactly, we liked them.  I wish I knew what happened or how I can reach them.

I ended up having my case sent to another attorney and was told there was nothing even done when my case was with ripley. Now, I question why they needed my ssn.  They also took an entire month to send my case to the new attorney and only sent it when I called the new lawyer and, in turn, they called ripleys office for it. The file was never sent. I would advise to go elsewhere. Beyond unprofessional. I will also be reporting this to the michigan bar association so this won't happen to anyone else.

reviews.text:
A few months back I had finally got the courage up to leave a mentally and physically abusive relationship. I reached out for help regarding custody/child support and was paired with Jim Boerigter.  I paid my 300$ fee for an appointment that was set for a Thursday morning at 10 am. I did not receive the call until 10:20 am. He asked me a couple of general questions which included the ages of my children. Noticing that they were year after year he proceeded to ask me if I ”knew how getting pregnant worked”. He degraded me by saying ”Now you are going to be riding off of me and the rest of the taxpayers for however long”.
-You must start with compassion and empathy to truly understand that “but for the grace of God go I” applies to every client.
-You must have an understanding of basic human behavior in times of emotional, mental, and sometimes even physical trauma that lets you further grasp the nature of your client’s real issues.
-Of course, you must also possess an acute knowledge of the law and the court system to explain to your client how the legal process works and what you will do to help them through the process to achieve a fair and reasonable result under the circumstances.
These are just a few of the bullet points under his information online. It was also stated in his profile that he is extremely involved in Pro Bono work, that he is ”a lawyer for the people” and helps provide legal assistance for those who would otherwise not be able to afford it. Yet I paid 300$ And had no intention of asking for Pro Bono work. He put me down for needing assistance being a single mom, I was humiliated for attempting to better myself with furthering my education. He made the statement that ”I alone had caused this situation”. Not only did I feel degraded but I felt his legal advice was off. My case involves making changes due to multiple reasons including physical and mental abuse. He suggested dropping the case to avoid court. If I were to drop the case I feel it would seem that I possibly made false allegations or that I changed my mind about protecting the children. I feel it would discredit me. Again, I am not educated in law but it seemed off.
I cried through the entire conversation and he never seemed to try to let up or show true sympathy for my situation. He would say he wasn’t trying to be rude but wouldn’t stop the rude comments. He knew I had been physically and mentally abused because It was stated in the information I sent before the consultation. It was also brought to his attention that I am pregnant yet he seemed to continue to be harsh with me at my emotional state. When our call ended I called a friend to vent about the situation and started balling so hard I thought I was going to pass out. My emotions had nothing to do with getting disappointing legal information but everything to do with the way I was treated. I honestly wish I had recorded the entire conversation because it was extremely unprofessional and inappropriate.
Being involved in family law I feel this man should be educated on being sympathetic towards victims of both physical and mental abuse. He should be aware of the abuse cycle and realize people who get stuck in this cycle come from all ethnicities and backgrounds. Abuse is found in all income and education levels. I felt judged and scrutinized. I am very disappointed with the outcome of my consultation and will most certainly not be moving forward with this attorney.
reviews.ownerResponse: We’re sorry you had a negative experience when contacting our firm. We try to treat each caller with respect, empathy, and professionalism.

I also recommended him to my brother, who was very pleased with his trust/will services as well. Thank you again Atty Howell, you're the best!... 5 STARS!

My dad and I hired Oliver Howell in 2021 to help us with my grandfather's trust. My uncle took over as sole trustee when my mom passed and he thought all of the property of the trust was now his. Once Oliver got involved he started filing in the court system for us to get our personal property from the trust property first. It took over a year in order to finally get our property from the trust. Unfortunately on the day and time agreed upon, we had issues getting our property due to my uncle not willing to give us all of our stuff even though the court order said we could take it. I had to contact Oliver on a Saturday while at the trust property trying to get our stuff loaded up. Oliver spent a few hours contacting my uncle's lawyer firm to get this resolved. Long story short we had 6 court hearings in 2022 and Oliver was fully prepared for each one. Oliver would also give suggestions based on his experience that might be best for our situation. No matter our decision, Oliver always did the best he could to get us a favorable decision from the court system. I highly recommend his services if you have estate/trust issues. Oliver listens to the situation and does his best for his clients.

Oliver Howell has been amazing in taking care of our trust needs.  He has explained clearly and concisely alternatives, making sure we have a full understanding of choices.  His integrity and follow through have been all that can be expected.  I would highly recommend him for any necessary litigation.

I highly recommend Mr. Oliver Howell as a skilled and knowledgeable attorney.  His professionalism during a difficult process was invaluable.  In addition, he is down-to-earth and has a great sense of humor.  This is important to me.  He always made me feel comfortable with the questions I was asking and took the time to explain each step of the process.

I sought the services of Mr. Howell after the passing of my father, as I have no experience with estates, probate court, or the like. In every aspect Mr. Howell has showed exemplary professionalism and skill, and has made this process as easy and painless as possible. I would definitely recommend Mr. Howell and the services of Howell, Parfet, Schau.
Oliver and his firm recently performed my trust work.  Timely, professional, and well done.  Highly recommend their services.
Oliver made setting up our estate plan easy and stress-free

Mr. Howell is a superiorly knowledgable attorney who is a partner in a well-resourced law firm in Kalamazoo.  Mr. Howell has represented and counseled me with regard to: Michigan trustee and personal representative fiduciary roles and responsibilities; trustee defense from litigious beneficiaries; execution of trust documents; counsel to Michigan estate laws; and, my personal estate planning and trust creation. I have recommended Mr. Howell to my business associates in Kalamazoo, MI and they too have retained Mr. Howell and his firm: Howell Parfet Schau. His firm’s partners, associates, and paralegals are diligent and knowledgeable. Mr. Howell is courteous, professionally mature, well versed in litigation of Michigan probate laws and court procedures, and most importantly he keeps his client continuously updated and responds to correspondence within hours. Mr. Howell remains my forever counsel and I highly recommend him for your probate responsibilities and estate planning.

Oliver has been instrumental in our real estate business. He is
down to earth, professional, knowledgeable and easy to work with.
I highly recommend!!
Oliver Howell was very professional and friendly helping us generate our Trust. Please reach out to him and get yours done before it’s too late.

Oliver Howell of Howell Parfet Schau was exceptional in handling our case and obtaining a settlement in our favor.  It was a difficult case with multiple defendants, and Oliver navigated the situation with great skill. Oliver succeeded where a previous law firm was ineffective in obtaining a settlement.

Oliver Howell of Howell, Parfet, & Schau handled the sale of our home to a private party without using any realtors.  Oliver was terrific from the first contact.  We were extremely impressed with how quickly Oliver responded to messages.  He gave us an outline of the information he needed us to provide based on our negotiations with the buyer, then took our responses and crafted a perfect buy/sell agreement within three days.  He responded to our questions and concerns on weekends and evenings.  He also made arrangements for a title company to handle the closing, contacting them during the process to keep things moving. At one point, we had a road block because a mortgage from the 1990’s had not been discharged even though it had been paid before 2000.  Oliver worked with the Title Company to make sure that was resolved.  He reviewed all the closing work papers for us before we completed the sale.  In summary, he made a difficult process easy for us and saved us tens of thousands of dollars.
Oliver is fantastic to work with. He is very knowledgeable and gives sound advice that is was very helpful for our needs.

reviews.text:
I am a physician and applied for an exemption during for the COVID vaccine during the period the federal government threatened to withhold CMS funding to hospitals refusing to implement a vaccine mandate.

I called at least 5 different law firms, and none would take up my case; they frankly seemed afraid to be associated with it.

My initial consultation call was over 30 minutes and extremely helpful and, given that I was facing unemployment and was told I’d be blacklisted from the field, uplifting.

Ultimately after working with them, I was initially placed on three weeks of paid leave during the holiday season and my hospital privileges were renewed with an exemption in place.

Even if I had lost, I would have been satisfied with this firm; they were tedious, well-prepared, reasonably priced, communicative, and approachable.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for sharing your story. We’re so glad our team was able to support you during a difficult time. Your kind words are appreciated and we’re truly grateful that you chose our law firm to support you.

My family has used the “Butler, Toweson & Payseno – P.L.L.C.” law firm for many capacities.  Their attorneys, legal aids, and staff have helped us properly address wills, powers of attorney, estate settlements, etc. and have always done a superb job at resolving our needs.  I recently had a mental breakdown which allowed me to be charged with a crime uncharacteristic of myself.  With my mind in panic mode, I knew the best place to go for help was “Butler, Toweson & Payseno – P.L.L.C.”.  The firm’s criminal attorney, Anthony Toweson, worked through the system to have the formal arrest, arraignment, and release on a PR Bond processed swiftly.  His experience and knowledge of the court systems in Kalamazoo County as well as the contacts he has established helped move the process forward and negotiate a reduced charge and final sentencing.  I learned that I could trust Mr. Toweson and believe in his ability to resolve the unfortunate situation in which I found myself.  With their broad scope of legal services, I would not hesitate to go back to “Butler, Toweson & Payseno – P.L.L.C.” for any future legal issues, great or small.
Roy - Satisfied Client

reviews.text: Getting a driver's license reinstated can be a intimidating and daunting task. When the time came, Mr Toweson's presentation of my case at my hearings was amazing, and he made my part in them easy. We won both hearings, first getting a restricted license back, and then ultimately getting my full license restored. Throughout the process, I was treated with nothing but respect and understanding, and given the support to succeed. I could not recommend Tony Toweson, and his office, any more strongly than I do here. Good luck to all.

Client Testimonial: Our Family’s Deepest Appreciation for Willis Law
Willis Law – Kalamazoo, Michigan
Attorneys: Sam Gilbertson & Davis C. Martin
When our family needed legal guidance during some of the hardest moments of our lives, Willis Law stood beside us with a level of integrity, compassion, and professionalism we will never forget.
________________________________________
Part I: Willis Law’s Support After Our Mother’s Passing (2018–2022)
When our mother passed in 2018, our family was inexperienced with legal matters and unprepared for the challenges that followed. What should have been a time of healing quickly became a period of grief, stress, and confusion.
Soon after her death, we discovered that two younger siblings had persuaded our 84-year-old mother to sign a Quit Claim Deed transferring our entire 161-acre family farm solely to them. This was completely opposite of what our parents had always told us—that everything they worked for was meant to be shared equally among their children. The discovery was shocking and deeply painful.
Not knowing where to turn, we reached out to Willis Law, and that call changed the course of everything.
Attorney Sam Gilbertson became our guide during one of the most difficult family disputes we have ever faced. Sam combined deep legal expertise with honesty, patience, and kindness. He helped us navigate a long, emotionally charged battle with siblings who refused to cooperate.
Through years of delays and resistance, Sam remained steady and determined. He stayed focused on what was right and fair. In the end, the court ruled that the property should be sold and the proceeds divided equally among all heirs—an outcome that brought justice and restored a sense of fairness to our family.
Without Sam’s commitment, persistence, and professionalism, this resolution would not have been possible.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for taking the time to share such a detailed and heartfelt reflection on your experience. We are truly honored by your trust.

Losing a loved one is difficult on its own, and facing unexpected legal challenges during that time can be overwhelming. We are grateful that you turned to Willis Law during such a critical moment, and we appreciate your recognition of the work Attorney Sam Gilbertson put into guiding your family through a long and emotionally complex matter.

Sam’s dedication to integrity, fairness, and thorough advocacy is central to how we serve our clients. It is meaningful to hear that his commitment brought clarity, stability, and ultimately justice during an incredibly challenging period for your family.

Thank you again for placing your confidence in our firm. We remain committed to serving you with the utmost professionalism should you ever need assistance in the future.

Throughout the proceedings, Joe and his team were always responsive to my questions and concerns, often replying to emails within hours.

What I valued most was his candid guidance and unwavering support as we navigated the divorce process together. He was not just my attorney but also a source of support and guidance.

If you are going through a divorce, I highly recommend Joseph Vander Horst.

He enabled me to weather a complicated and tumultuous process and in my opinion went above and beyond. He zeroes in on what truly matters and avoids the nonsense. A very friendly and professional lawyer who I cannot recommend highly enough!

If I ever am unfortunate enough to do this twice, I'll go back to Joe!

I’ve used Joe Vanderhorst for a couple different family law and custody issues over the last 5 years! Joe is incredibly networked and experienced in family law in this area! He has never steered me wrong or inflated my expectations going into a legal battle. I value my family more than anything else and I would willingly place them in Joe’s hands again should I need to!

Jon on multiple occasions went above and beyond expectations for me, and I’m very grateful. He was very understanding and a great team player, he took his game plan and mine, merged them together and the outcome was amazing! I told him exactly what I wanted and he made it happen, just unbelievable! And the secretaries have been very helpful also when it comes to contacting him, nothing but very positive and effective results from this firm, STRONGLY RECOMMEND!!
We have been taking care of my Aunt Donna's thing and Micheal has been very helpful
Amazing, amazing people. Joe V was a godsend. Very personable and worked very hard for me. Toni, just as amazing!

Horrible experience with lawyers Joseph Vanderhorst and his unpleasant secretary Audra. I have been fighting an ongoing battle for years to keep joint custody of my kids. My ex wife continuously tries to inch away at my time in order to get more child support, so I decided to try a more strategic approach by involving a lawyer (Vanderhorst). In the beginning I told him I want half-joint custody like I originally had and he agreed after reviewing my case. That would be the first and last cooperation we would have together. The very first court date I was thrown off guard by him trying to get me to compromise to have less time than half (only 2 days) time with my kids and his big motivator was that my child support would drop down despite my original plan. Of course my ex seen through his fast talking and declined his attempts to end our case then and there, ie: the reason I told him I wanted half custody- my original order and we’ll probably have to fight, whole purpose of getting a lawyer.... I can go back and forth with my ex myself... instead of paying 2,500$ for someone to mediate. Oh and the 2,500$ originally started with a quote of 1500$ then after office visit he said it was a mistake. After first court date, and the entirety of his employment I seen no results but was steadily receiving new charges in the mail.... I mean ridiculous charges, 200$ for a email here and there that I had no knowledge of or received no information about... 100$ for gas up the road... 100$ for a phone call... 200$ for signing some paperwork. The total charges end up being close to 4000$ dollars, I still have seen no results, in fact I’m worst off now in debt, I fired him Due to lack of results, all talk and charging. His secretary Audra was very disrespectful as well, she acted as if she’s doing you a favor by taking his calls and at one point (which made me terminate services finally), she became a conflict of interest in my case, I felt as if she was representing my ex. Anyhow $4000 dollars later still in the same spot and paying the max in child support, while I have my kids only 2 days a week I still pay as if I never have them, and Joseph Vanderhorst couldn’t even tackle that. What a joke, more money for his pocket to drive around with in his Mercedes. I’m positive his whole agenda is to take your money and end your case quickly as possible even if it means sacrificing your rights and wants. Wish there were negative stars on the rating.
Helping plan our future.

Attorney Steven Weston & Paralegal Sara Burr were the dream team for me in a season of my life that was nothing short of a nightmare. In a time where you don't know up from down, it was a VERY secure feeling knowing I had them both in my corner. It was imperative for me to have an attorney that had the integrity that Steve HAS and SHOWCASED through my time of being their client as a victim of a drunk driving accident.

When tragedy struck my family.....the first attorney I thought of calling was Steve Weston. Every time I observed him with ICU families he was ALWAYS kind, professional and ethical. Words cannot express how thankful I am to have had Steve working for me to make sure the hit and run driver who killed my son was held accountable. The driver’s v insurance company fought at every process to keep from paying any viable amount. Steve never backed down and was able to stand up to the insurance company and get a fair settlement. The level of compassion, caring and support was amazing and I will always be grateful for their help through this horrendous journey. I would strongly recommend Steve Weston as a highly respected and effective attorney for any case you need a strong professional fighting for you!

reviews.text:
I've never heard back from them pertaining to my problems with my apartment complex. I contacted them after not hearing back from either of the Ordinance Enforcement Officers for Oshtemo Township on
7275 W. Main Street, Kalamazoo, MI 49009. I've emailed them twice since I was flooded by my neighbors sewage flooding my basement and countless attempts to my landlord to fix damages after I moved in. In November of last year I had an Enforcement Officer and a member of management out to do a walk through of my apartment. I expressed my concerns that I haven't had a lock on my back slider since I moved in. I also explained that there has been 4 floods on my basement up to that point 3 of which were caused by a single problem of a pipe from the A/C being clogged. When explaining this particular situation I was told to build shelves to protect my property. I was flabbergasted when the Enforcement Officer said that's how he prevents this in his home. I asked if he owned or rented, he owns. I pay rent and if I would have received prompt service from experienced maintenance I wouldn't need to go without a water heater for 3 days or have to purchase 2 dehumidifiers and and industrial fan to keep the basement dry and clean. Either way I wish someone would call me back because I've been going through a lot and only receive letters from my landlord about violations pertaining to my dogs which are emotional support animals. I've received 3 letters over the last year and each coordinate with a complaint I have with maintenance. I also have received 2 notices for 2 of my cars one had a flat tire and the other was expired plats for a car I wasn't using (my college son's car). I've looked through the parking lots and have seen disabled vehicles on top of not being registered that have been that way for months without a notice attached to those vehicles. I need help please. I've never paid my rent late, I receive a payment on the 3rd of the month to which my rent is automatically charged that day since move in 2 years ago
Great advice

reviews.text: I have had the pleasure of retaining both Chris Lalonde and Darren Malek for a multitude of things from estate planning and trust administration to litigation work and they offer expert level legal advice and counsel on all fronts. I do not hesitate to recommend their services to anyone needing legal assistance.
Chris has done so much for us. Wold trust them with any legal issue we had. Thanks !!!!
Great job!!

They have my grandmothers estate fully liquidated and refuse to release the money for over a year with only excuses like “next week” but next week was months ago.  Now they won’t even answer my calls or hang up on me.  Rude and unprofessional at best.
Chris and his staff helped us make our life long dream come true.
A great law firm for services ranging from business advice to estate planning to professional practice transitions. Plus Veritas Law Group has 3 great locations to serve you.
I know Mr. Stancati is retired as of 6 months ago. But not only was he a wonderful and insightful man. He was an amazing divorce attorney. I am sure the rest of his staff is as well.
Caring lawyers that get things done.

I would like to say Kal. Co's best attorney however I will go one step further, SW MI's best attorney is R.Stancati. He has gone above and beyond to help with a family law situation over the past 4 years. When I heard he was a "bulldog" that actually hardly covers it. Years of experience and knowledge, always on top of his game and witty! Having close family/friend relationships with those in the law/legal community I had high expectations, Ross came recommended to me by another local attorney, and he did not disappoint. I have recommended him multiple times to others in need of family law guidance. I have put a lot of trust into him, just knowing he is only a phone call away. Of course, the office staff is amazing as well, the ladies are also very knowledgeable and always on top of things! Thank you to the whole office for everything over the past few years.
Ross Stancati is truly a genius! His representation is stellar! Kalamazoo is fortunate to have such an amazing person/lawyer in the community! I highly recommend him and all associates!

Allison is a very professional, knowledgeable, friendly, helpful and excellent attorney.  I would recommend her to anyone who may be needing bankruptcy or domestic relations advice.  She is very pleasant to deal with.  She is also very responsive to her clients.
Attorney Allison Greenlee Korr is amazing "hands down"

In very difficult cases, or even simple cases your going to want the best attorney you can find with good rates, I'll be happy to inform you that your search is over. Call Allison Greenlee Korr for a free consultation.
I first hired Allison Greenlee Korr in 2011 at which point she won my case. I still need her from time to time and as always she's much help. Allison definitely puts her clients first.

Ross Stancati is very well know as "Kalamazoo's Most Trusted" Family Law Attorney. He has proven time and time again over the last 40 years to Win complicated cases. Father's do have RIGHT'S in the State of Michigan, especially when he Represents You, for the sake of your children. Its worth the consultation and visit. His office of attorneys is well diversified and Specialize especially in Family and Custody situations. They also do Estate Planning, Real Estate, Business and much more..
Michelle represented me some years ago against my childs mother who had tried to run off with my child.  She got me full custody and it was all over within 90 days

The WORST experience I've ever had. I will be coming back to leave a breakdown of my experience once my new lawyer looks over the breakdown of cost. Just for an idea of her unethical business practices, I was NOT able to list or sell my home  unless I used HER husband instead of my realtor that I was already committed to prior. She went from $5000.00 to needing another $3000 then asked for Another $5000 before I dismissed her from representing me.   I will be back to share my full experience and honest opinion once my new lawyer looks over all of the cost & charges.

3 minutes ago

Michelle's reaction was strange when opposing counsel reached out to her to verify her client's paystubs/source of income in a child support case. She became defensive and flat out refused. When Michelle realized that opposing counsel filed a subpoena to her client's employer(they were hiding two new employments and an increase in salary to rig the child support calculation to get more money), she then suddenly provided new paystubs from another job that matched the information uncovered from the subpoena previously filed. If you have nothing to hide as a lawyer, why would you refuse to verify your client's paystubs? Throughout the entire child support case, Michelle relayed 4 different health insurance premiums, and when asked to provide the documentation for verification, she stalled. Her uncooperative behavior during a simple child support calculation led opposing counsel to discover that her client was defrauding the opposing party, and therefore the court to get a higher child support amount than legally entitled to. The outrageous thing is Michelle's reaction during the process when confronted about the fact that the paystubs her client sent several times were fraudulent. There is one thing to defend a client, but refusing to verify documents provided in a discovery process for a legal matter looks sketchy for a lawyer.

Responding to Michelle's comment : The point of my review was to highlight a serious lapse in ethical responsibility. Presenting undated paystubs during a child support case, which concealed a substantial income increase is simply fraudulent. I am not sure how this could be considered a compliment, but I believe it is important for others to be aware of these practices before hiring you a lawyer as they will get in serious legal troubles and damage their credibility or reputation.

I also want to clarify that my review was not intended as a compliment(thinking otherwise would be a public direct statement of your own character), but rather to raise concern over a serious issue that occurred during a child support case. Specifically, the use of undated paystubs played a role in concealing a significant increase in the opposing party's income, something that came to light only after further legal proceedings.

While I understand that attorneys advocate for their clients, I believe there is a line between advocacy and withholding information that could impact a fair outcome, especially in matters involving children. My goal in writing this review is to inform others of my experience so they can make fully informed decisions when choosing legal representation.

reviews.text: Jody and Michelle were amazing! Very patient and communicative throughout the process.

reviews.text:
Michelle was recommended to me but I wish I had chosen the other attorney.

She is knowledgeable of the law, she is kind, BUT she told me what I wanted to hear (RED FLAG). I was very excited to get things started regarding the feedback from Michelle I had received regarding the folders of evidences I had organized for her. She seemed a good fit. Unfortunately, a few weeks into working with McIntyre Law I realized this is just another example of willingness to take cases without enough time, or manpower, or resources or something! It was clear I was not a priority to her or her firm my entire case. It became more clear as 6mo to a year into the process Michelle was asking (emailing and charging you!) about simple items regarding my case..

Also, the paralegal, Jodi, took almost all of my words from a feedback document I returned to her and used them in a later court document to be filed. Some of this document used profanity. I was appalled to see what had been filed. I bite my tongue as words cannot be taken back..
Confirmed and apologized by Michelle. They still charged me $$$$ for that document.

I felt like this attorney had her own agenda as to how this case would be handled, versus my plea to her for filing and fighting for 50-50..(meanwhile other side is offering 2 additional days versus the contested 7)

She pushed and pushed and pushed me to settle when I wanted badly to fight for my son and evidence I spent 5 years working on.

Come to find out, FROM MICHELLE, it is extremely hard to change custody- “your evidence really isn’t enough to convince the court beyond reasonable doubt..” yet she could never really give me an example of evidence to try and find. It was a hard situation, I understand, but my evidence didn’t change from the beginning to the end and I think that was a huge mistake in this case.

Michelle told me she doesn't guarantee anything but sees a case with the great evidence/messages/pictures, organization, receipts, notes etc. She took advantage of me and my situation, she could have easily told me this isn't much of a case and turned me away. Turns out, she just did that at the end when she had my $$$$.

I felt uninformed, underequipped, and over charged using this attorney. I am 1 year and $$$$ into my case and I have not even had a hearing due to tons of adjournments, misunderstandings, and lack of follow through.  Did I mention she bailed on me and my case last week at the last minute (week of the hearing). Fortunately, she did forgive the cost for her to file her withdrawal paperwork and small ending balance she worked up.

Overall, very poor and expensive experience working with McIntyre Law. I am in a more defeated and vulnerable position in gaining more time with my son than when I began.
reviews.ownerResponse: Gavin it is very disingenuous of you to write this because I was honest with you relative to the evidence that you had.  You paid me to provide legal advice and that is what I did even though it may be information you didn’t want to hear. I even waived a substantial amount of outstanding fees which was well over 1,000.  I wish you the best despite your inaccurate review. Badmouthing your attorney is not the answer.  Sometimes the best approach is to take a step back and heed the advice.

Best Criminal Defense Law Firm in Southwest Michigan
Jon is a well rounded and knowledgeable attorney who is a delight to work with.

Not a great experience and don't recommend him.  Other 10 ratings were 5 star so not sure what was different.
Jonathan seems knowledgeable and our things started off well.  We provided lots of documentation, records, descriptions, etc. for our personal injury Case.
However, some key scenarios were not communicated as options.  We did get a settlement but our health insurer then put a lien on it.  This was never communicated as a possibility, but once it happened, Jonathan said this was always possible.  He took his cut of the settlement from the top so got full payment.  As a result, our settlement was greatly reduced and he actually received more money from it than we did.  Also, communication was very minimal as our Case progressed.
This was our 1st (and hopefully last) legal Case and it was very frustrating.  We recommend thorough evaluation of representation options and likely a different attorney.
Jonathan made planning our estate an easy task to do.  We would recommend him for all your auto and motorcycle accidents and expecially for your estate planning.
Helped us tremendously, I would recommend Jonathan to everyone.

My hometown, it has a little bit of everything.
Nice small city.

I've recommended them to clients & businesses but no more.

We hired them to facilitate the process of getting an H2A Visa for a family member. Started the process at the beginning of the year, they never follow up, deadlines were always waited until the last possible moment, forms rejected, and contant "...well because of Covid we're" backlogged, short staffed, whatever their excuse was.  Being short staffed & backlogged sure didn't stop them from getting THEIR invoices out on time but they didnt have a care in the world to do our work in a timely manner.   Here we are in October/November and nothing was accomplished & they called to say "Oops, visas are all gone for the year.  Good luck, bye bye."  What a waste of money, stress & aggravation.

I hired Jason Colvin to represent me as recommended by a friend.  He did not get me any better deal than a public defender would have, refused to show the evidence I had, convinced me that pleading guilty would be the best decision, and refused to advocate for me.  Jason also failed to tell me he doesn’t normally work in the county my offense was in until we were at court, then told me he had never really worked with that prosecutor.  Jason also failed to even get the evidence that the court “allegedly” had against me.  Jason maybe spent a total of 5 hours on my case.  I paid the retainer which was over $1,000, and I get a call almost a year later claiming I owe an additional $3,000.  This place definitely taking advantage of people and it’s not OK.

Did not tell my Mother, who had a Trust made through Miller-Johnson, that it would cost another $5000.00 to administer the trust. She had already paid over $5000.00 to draw up the trust.
My Mother recently passed. March 2, 2020.
Our family does NOT HAVE $5000.00 to convert Trust.
Shame on you for taking advantage of and charging people outrageous prices. Please consult other Lawyers before using this company.
Nice people

Marc represented me and my wife in an immigration case involving adjustment of status from F-1 via a marriage-based Petition for Alien Relative (Form I-130) plus others relevant to our case. He approached it with professional rigor throughout, and was very understanding of our concerns and panic of uncertainty throughout the process.

He has courtroom experience and a deep understanding of appeals. His attention to procedural nuance and risk mitigation is far beyond standard form-prep services. A smart person would think they can handle these forms, but there is important nuance to building a successful petition. Every document was checked with exacting care, and he anticipates many potential issues before they arise, avoiding costly or risky delays.

If your case has any complexity, non-trivial immigration history, procedural uncertainty, or risk of heightened scrutiny he is the level of representation you need. His preparation reflects litigation-level standards, and I think it will make a measurable difference in our outcome.
Marc was very helpful and quick to respond.  I would highly recommend him to any friend or family

I am so happy that Zac & Ancella Briggs had finally found a lawyer who knows what to do. I had attended their wedding back in 2015 & but it took them more than 1 lawyer & 9 years later where the spouse visa was finally approved!!! Thanks Marc for finally uniting the couple in 1 country!!!

Highly recommend for immigration issues. He was straightforward and answered all the questions I had about my mother’s case at the initial consultation.

He really knows his immigration stuff just be sure to have all your documents and questions ready for your case.

We were trying to apply for a spouse visa. After trying to do it ourselves and got scammed by an immigration attorney, it was a blessing that we met Marc and it was the best decision we ever made to take him as our attorney

Process took about 1 year, yes it might seem long but the hold up was from our side. We were caught up with our daily life and didn't able to comply Marc's request according to his timeline, however, Marc is always patience with us

Once I got my interview schedule in my home country, Marc was willing to have video conference and did a trial interview with me

I've been in this country for almost a year and already got my green card. Every now and then I had some legal questions about legal documents and Marc is always able to answer my question thoroughly

Again, taking Marc as our attorney is the best decision ever. We met a lot of people that had issues with immigration and they all use Marc and they're successfully get what they need

Whenever we meet someone in need of an immigration lawyer, Marc is always on our speed dial

Marc is an attorney with questionable ethics, integrity, and honesty, took on an immigration case for my sister-in-law, who was dealing with the U.S. Embassy in Kinshasa. He claimed to have handled similar cases and requested $3,500 from a vulnerable African woman. However, once paid, he began avoiding her and neglected the case entirely.

Despite the urgency of the situation, Marc wasted months without doing any meaningful work. By August, he had made no progress and consistently avoided meeting with us, claiming he was sick. In total, he only spent 2.5 hours with her, and even though we sent him over 45 emails, he never responded.

Marc has refused to refund the money and caused my sister-in-law’s case to miss critical deadlines. As a WMU alumnus and former resident of Kalamazoo, Marc is taking advantage of immigrant families. Please do not trust his services—he's only interested in taking your money. We ended up working on the case ourselves and left him with the money, but we know God is seeing him. DO NOT PAY HIM FULLY BEFORE THE END OF THE CASE.
Marc is very knowledgeable, thorough and detail oriented.  He thinks of all contingencies and communicates effectively.  His generous competent care is priceless.

Response to Response from the owner:
I most certainly was a client of yours (see attached photo). Just because you do not remember me does not mean that you were not a bad lawyer to me. The reason why I posted a poor review is that you gave terrible advice that cost significant $$ over the course of my case. You were strongly against me changing my case out of state to the state that I moved to while my appeal played out, again, over 1400 miles away. Regardless of the verdict the best thing for your client, me, would have been to change my case to the state where I live (I'm poor, disabled, and travel is expensive). The best thing for my lawyer, you, would have been for me to keep things where they were. That's what you advised me to do, that's what I did, and it cost me plenty since then. Bad job. Trying to curate your reviews won't change these facts.
reviews.ownerResponse: I have no idea who you are or why you would post a poor review. I do not even believe you were a client at my office. I am certain I did not give the advice you have described. Though we do everything possible to help our clients, SSD cases are decided by Administrative Law Judges. We can’t guarantee a case will be approved. Furthermore, the review is inconsistent with hundreds of reviews outlining how much I care about my clients. Unfortunately, we consider your review to be false and defamatory in nature. We are requesting that you remove the review.

reviews.text: What an absolutely amazing attorney! Not many attorneys come with compassion. I know, as someone who has an employment history with courts and attorneys. Attorney Peter Schinkai was appointed to my case, 400 miles away. The distance between us never affected his ability to fight for me. He was both pleasant and extremely professional, at all times. I could not see the forest for the trees as I was personally involved, despite my knowledge. Peter could. And he fought to knock down everyone that stood in our way. It was a very long and difficult 90 days in my life. I started out facing charges just less than murder and ended with a dismissal. The result is nothing less than a miracle. It was Peter that did all the work. I will be eternally grateful for this amazing attorney for the rest of my life. The best part was that he believed me, from day one, and he fought for that truth. And today, that truth has set me free.  Thank you, forever, for giving me back my life, Peter Schinkai.

It is never too late to give a big thank you to Attorney Giffels.
I came to him through the reviews I read about him and even with my language impediment, since my native language is Spanish, he had no restrictions on attending to me and taking my case.

I always felt his support, confidence and I knew that no matter how much time passed, he would continue to fight for my case.
The results were not what I expected, but it was not his fault, rather it was the fault of the system and the other party being sued, but even so; I am infinitely grateful to him for supporting, considering, and helping me in this process.

I recommend the lawyer 1000 percent, and I urge my community to go to him when you find yourself in a similar situation, since you will find the support and the answer you want to hear.

reviews.text: Brain is very good attorney for divorce case, specially if there is business involved

Dear Brian,

My husband and I want to sincerely thank you from the bottom of our hearts. You took time out of your day to listen to us, offering the best advice possible. You could have easily charged us, but you didn’t—and that alone speaks volumes about the kind of attorney you are.

Our community needs more attorneys like you—someone who listens, understands, and genuinely cares. We know that not all cases are straightforward, especially when clients don’t always provide the full picture. Yet, you took the time to hear my story completely and offered invaluable guidance with honesty and integrity.

There are no words to fully express how much we appreciate you. Thank you again for everything.

Sincerely,
Danny Frigo

I highly recommend Justin Workman to anyone seeking legal assistance. His professionalism and dedication make him an outstanding attorney,

I cannot recommend Steve McLaughlin highly enough. From the very beginning, he treated my case with professionalism, care, and genuine understanding. Navigating the legal system can be overwhelming, especially in a long and emotionally difficult case, but Steve consistently made me feel supported, informed, and confident throughout the entire process.

He is extremely knowledgeable, thorough, and dedicated, and always took the time to clearly explain every step so I understood exactly what was happening. What truly sets him apart is his compassion and willingness to go above and beyond for his clients. During my case, he was able to help me receive approval to take my honeymoon cruise outside of the United States — something that meant the world to me and my family, and something I will always be deeply grateful for.

Steve is not only an outstanding attorney, but also a genuinely kind and understanding person. If you are looking for a lawyer who will fight for you, treat you with respect, and truly care about your outcome, I wholeheartedly recommend Steve McLaughlin and his firm. Amazing team!
reviews.ownerResponse:
So glad Steve was able to help with the Honeymoon, especiall! Steve tells me it was fantastic. Steve was a long time prosecutor that I did battle with many times. I know his character - that is why he is with us today, as demonstrated by your thoughtful and kind words; they mean more to Steve, and us as a team, than you know. Thank you and we all wish you well.
Mike

reviews.text: Arden Mitchell was my attorney and I could not ask for anyone better!  His understanding and compassion during this stressful time I went through was amazing. He treated me with the upmost respect and kindness.  Arden made sure I understood every step along the way.  He was truly amazing.  Thank you Arden!

This law firm is comprised of excellent professionals with the community's best interests in mind. My initial interactions with this law firm were with a highly knowledgeable, respectful, and detail-oriented attorney named Mr. Tony DeAgostino. Tony guided me through the most stressful and unfortunate circumstances my life has ever dealt me. He listened patiently to emotional distresses and redirected me from my anxieties, all while diligently addressing the legal procedures, formal discussions, and official requests that came with assuring a fair and just judgment in the judicial system.

After working with Tony and closing out the first of the pair of cases that fell upon me, several months passed where I was getting little help and little guidance in the Family Court judicial system. I was faced with an unjust binary choice and felt as though in the Criminal sector with Tony, I was represented to a degree that was demonstrably more focused on clear and evident client representation and the pursuit of justice. So, knowing that Hills at Law is a Criminal Defense firm, I reached back out to see if they would be able, or willing, to take up my case in the Family Circuit-- despite being a Criminal Defense Law Firm. Since they had handled the Criminal portion of this case, they were most intimately familiar with the substance, events, and details of this case, and hopefully, those items would serve well for the Family Circuit.

Lo and behold, I was graciously taken under the wing of Mr. Arden Mitchell-- another stellar, astute, cordial, and insightful Defense Attorney with the Hills at Law Firm. I retained Arden as my attorney after experiencing seven months of painstakingly slow and unmotivated representation in the Family Circuit. Arden took up my case and within three months of retention, we had mutually decided upon a strategy to demonstrate the necessary path forward in the court, achieved each of the milestones within the strategy, and achieved the desired outcome within the Family Circuit. Arden devoted much of his time to talking with me, explaining procedural formalities, and developing a plan to decisively communicate what the just decision is, and why this is the just decision.

I cannot say enough good things about this law firm. In addition to Tony's and Arden's great work, I also had only positive interactions with their assistants. Ms. Angel Morris was a pleasure to work with, offered kind reassurances, and was always available for a discussion, addressing questions, and providing situational clarity.

Again, cannot say enough to promote this law firm. Thank you all for sticking with me over this past year and a half.
Extremely helpful and professional. They made a stressful situation much easier to understand and were great to work with.
Very helpful and knowledgeable.
Great experience, 10/10 would recommend. Justin workman was awesome!

As a business owner of multiple companies within the area and as well as outside of the Michiana area. I have met 100+ thousand people across this planet. “Jessica Lafond” is without a doubt one of the smartest women I have ever met in my life. I can assure you that if you hire any other attorney you have already made your first mistake. If you want the best you have the best. Do not hesitate! Pick up that phone!

Eric Walker
CEO

I'm not one to write reviews...especially for lawyers.  However, Jessica LaFond is a such a force to be reckoned with.  Her intelligence, compassion, empathy and tenacity really sets the standard for any other attorney.  She represented my nephew who was in a horrible accident involving fatalities.  The kind of case in which no one really wins. Extremely emotional and devastating for all involved.  Her expertise of the law and humanity, really defines what every lawyer should strive to be. Seeing her defend my nephew in front of a jury, against 4 prosecutors was truly a David and Goliath moment. This whole law firm had such an emotional and professional investment with our family, and especially my nephew.  Jessica's  knowledge of the law and the eloquence in which she defends it, sets a standard unattainable by most attorneys.  Her light brightens the path of so many during their darkest walks in life.  Jessica's continued fight in the highly dysfunctional legal system, gives hope to the one’s lucky enough to have her on their side.   The whole firm really got involved and did their part to help Jessica.  Our family will be forever grateful.  Thank you Jessica and the Hill's Law firm for changing the way I view attorneys.
Hills law office has always been helpful to me in several different areas of law and I would recommend them to a friend or colleague.
Tony at hills is very professional and very good at his job
Tony did a remarkable job! He nipped my problem in the bud--I was facing a felony and he got my case completely dismissed. Bravo to Tony!

I had a great experience with Hills at law. Results from my case were better than expected. Justin Workman did an excellent job with my case and greatly appreciate all the dedication and hard work they put in. Communication and responses to questions for immediate and thorough. Would highly recommend them to handle your situation. Thanks Justin and Hills at law. Sincerely and thankful Brian

If I could give these people 0 stars I would. Not sure if they were just slow on business or what, but they tried to sell and scam me with their quote. I received a DWLS (Driving While License Suspended) and was quoted $3,500. Gave me the whole sales gimmick about having 8 people work on my case, etc. Started sending me text messages and emails trying to get me to sign some sort of documentation acknowledging that I would pay that.  Long story short I moved back to kzoo from Detroit, so decided to call this crazy expensive, but legit lawyer I knew. He quoted me $1,500 and he said this place is trying to scam me with their $3,500 quote. He said he is always the expensive guy and $1,500 is high, but $,3500 is outrageous.

I ended up going with Tony Toweson here in Parchment for $1,200. Got my case please down without talking to a judge and I paid $425 in total fines. He is gave me a free 10 minute consult so I could explain my situation and what I am hoping for. This SCAM of an attorney branch put some hot sounding young chick on the phone and they wouldn't go down a $1 in price, they wouldn't let me talk to an attorney for 5 mins nothing. This was the first place I called. I ended calling 5 different lawyers. Ranging from $1,000-$1,500 in quote. They were at $3,500. Don't get sold and scammed by this company. Make sure to call other places, they are just trying to money grab as many people as they can! Shame on you Hills At Law!
reviews.ownerResponse: I'm glad you found an attorney and your case worked out.  We are always striving to improve out intake process and will take your criticism to heart. However, our firm stands on it's reputation of hard work and excellent results.  If anyone thinks our firm is a scam, they have not looked very close.  Best of luck.  Mike.

Hills at Law are a comfortable affordable price (not cheap I do believe you get what you pay for) but they were under the quoted price of the other 80% other attorneys, I reached out to. Mind you I paid my first attorney 5,000 for my first offense and Kaitlyn got me the same exact outcome for my second offense! Most of the Attorneys, I reached out to practiced in divorce, traffic and criminal law etc but HIlls are strictly criminal law. Because of this they are very very knowledgeable at what they do and I do believe this is why my sentencing went so well. Highly recommend Kaitlyn and this law firm.

In the wake of a challenging situation resulting in personal injury and unresolved claims, I eventually found myself seeking legal counsel and assistance.
My introductory encounter with another law firm (which shall remain nameless) felt almost confrontational and left me with more questions than answers.
Then, thankfully, that initial disappointing and frustrating experience was turned around to become an entirely positive one, thanks to the great team at Conybeare Law.
I found attorney Nathaniel (Nate) Harrington to be a skilled, client-and-results focused professional, as well as being compassionate and accessible.
Kudos and gratitude additionally for the knowledgeable, outstanding support and follow-up I received from Windy and Amanda throughout the entire process.
I was truly satisfied with the experience and results from start to a finish and would recommend Conybeare Law.

reviews.text: I hired Anatasia Markou after talking to 3 different attorneys. Tase was direct and to the point. He laid out for me exactly what his plan was pre-charge, all the other attorneys wanted to wait until charges were filed.  He was aggressive with my case, and always communicated with me at every step. Once charges were filed he adjusted his strategy and spoke on my behalf to the prosecutor, trying to negotiate a plea deal. No plea deal was offered that was acceptable to me, so we went to preliminary trial. Tase sat down with me, explained all of my options, what his plan was, and what questions he was going to ask the state's witness. He had multiple pages of notes, which gave me great comfort knowing he was prepared and ready to fight for me! During preliminary trial the state's witnesses testimony was different than the police reports, and Tase knew it! He once again adjusted his strategy and threw out all his notes that he had wotked so hard on, and followed his gut! He questioned the witness thoroughly and locked the witness into their testimony. Tase did a fabulous job of cross examining them and as a result the judge threw the case out due to a lack of evidence. Without Tase's experience and gut instinct, that never would have happened! It was because of Tase that this case was not bound over for trial, causing me even more stress and heartache, and potentially a guilty verdict! If you need a lawyer that will fight for you with decades of experience, Anastasia Markou is the only one to choose! He will tell it to you straight and fight for you every step of the way! You only get one chance to choose the right lawyer and Tase is that man!
reviews.ownerResponse: THANKS ARIC FOR THE GREAT REVIEW!! Tase is a master strategist and very accomplished at cross examination. It is very rare to have a district Court judge DISMISS serious criminal charges following testimony at preliminary exam. This is just another example of how hard he works for the folks he represents. I am pleased that everything worked out so well for you. I am also grateful for your acknowledgement of Tase’s dedication. All the best. Randall Levine

reviews.text:
Exceptionally Skilled, Compassionate, and Respected — A Truly Outstanding Attorney

After interviewing 15 different attorneys, Sarissa K. Montague was the only one who called me back personally — and right away. From the very first conversation, I knew she was different. She was warm, compassionate, and genuinely took the time to understand our situation. I had a lot of questions, and she patiently answered every single one — always kind, never rushed, and completely thorough.

She asked us to come into her office because, as she explained, meeting in person helps her fight the case more effectively. That personal connection made all the difference. She took the time to get to know my son, and began preparing immediately with an incredible level of detail and dedication.

My son was facing serious charges — OWI refusal to blow and simple assault — in a very conservative county. Sarissa didn’t miss a thing. She carefully reviewed every aspect of the case, including the arrest video, and uncovered important facts — like how the accident was hard to avoid — that completely shifted the narrative.

At sentencing, her argument was so creative and impactful that even the judge commented on it. You could see the respect she commands in the courtroom — not just for her legal knowledge, but for her professionalism, preparation, and presence. Her reputation truly worked in our favor.

She also guided my son into counseling and made sure the court saw the whole picture — not just the charges. She fought not only for the best legal outcome but also for his future.

If you’re looking for an attorney who is incredibly skilled, deeply compassionate, and committed to every detail of your case, Sarissa K. Montague is the one you want. She always returns calls, always keeps you informed, and always gives your case her full attention.

She made a lasting difference in our lives, and I will truly miss her.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for the wonderful review.We are VERY FORTUNATE to have Sarissa on our criminal defense team.She is very skillful and always gets a good result for her clients.Your situation is no exception. I’m so glad it worked out so well for your family!! All the best .Randall Levine

reviews.text: Levine & Levine represented me from 2018 until 2024.  In 2021, I was wrongfully charged by a politically motivated Attorney General for crimes arising from the Flint Water Crisis in my capacity as Senior Advisor to then Governor Rick Snyder.  Initially, my interaction with Randy Levine was limited to depositions and civil lawsuit proceedings.  When charges were brought, Randy and Anastase Marko represented me from indictment all the way through an ultimate ruling by the State's Supreme Court dictating that I was erroneously charged and all charges must be dropped.  I found their legal expertise to be absolutely outstanding and even superior to attorneys from the largest most prestigious law firms.  They are strategic, relentless, creative and give 100% to their clients.  When the State of Michigan said they would reimburse my legal fees if I selected a different firm, I elected to keep Levine as counsel at my own expense.  As a recent editorial columnist stated in The Detroit News, "A guy with a 40 year sentence hanging over his head is not going to shop for legal services at Costco".  If Levine & Levine decides to take your case, I can absolutely guarantee you will have the very best in legal advocacy services.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you Rich!! The governments decision to prosecute you and governor Snyder for “crimes” allegedly committed during the Flint Water Crisis was a travesty!! Although it took us several years of contentious litigation against Attorney General Nessel’s army of prosecutors we were ultimately successful. The total DISMISSAL of serious criminal charges against you by the Michigan Supreme Court was one of the most satisfying victories Tase Markou , Sarissa Montague and I have had in our careers!! You served our citizens honorably !!You  did not deserve to be vilified by a political prosecutor who thought she would try to mollify the justifiably angry people of Flint by shifting the blame for the negligence of others on to you and former governor Snyder. Good luck to you in all of your  future endeavors.

Her deep knowledge of the law, strategic mindset, and relentless dedication were apparent at every stage. She communicated clearly, guided me through every step, and stood by me with strength and compassion.
